error_message: GitHub Pages deploy not working — site not loading
root_cause: Two issues: (1) vite.config.js had base: 'Neal-Konganda-Website' missing leading/trailing slashes, causing all asset paths to be wrong on the subpath deploy. (2) No GitHub Actions workflow existed, so the dist/ folder (gitignored) was never built and deployed.
fix: Fixed base to '/Neal-Konganda-Website/' with proper slashes. Added .github/workflows/deploy.yml using actions/upload-pages-artifact + actions/deploy-pages pattern. Also requires GitHub Pages source set to 'GitHub Actions' in repo Settings > Pages.

error_message: 13x 404 errors: profile.png, logos/*.svg, thumbs/*.svg all fail to load in dev and prod
root_cause: All public asset paths were hardcoded as root-absolute strings (e.g. '/profile.png'). With base: '/Neal-Konganda-Website/' in vite.config.js, Vite serves assets at '/Neal-Konganda-Website/profile.png' but the code requested '/profile.png' — a 404 in both dev and prod.
fix: Replaced all hardcoded public asset paths with import.meta.env.BASE_URL prefix (e.g. `${import.meta.env.BASE_URL}profile.png`). Vite substitutes BASE_URL with the configured base at build time. Also updated ANIM_FNS keys in ProjectThumb.jsx to use BASE_URL so the src→animFn lookup still works.

error_message: Backdrop dots only appear at the top of the page; bottom of viewport is empty
root_cause: flowOffset is an unbounded accumulator: each scroll event adds rawDelta * PARALLAX_FACTOR to targetFlow, which flowOffset lerps toward. After scrolling, flowOffset is a large positive number. All node drawn positions are node.y + flowOffset — since node.y is clamped to [0, h] by toroidal wrapping but flowOffset is unbounded, most nodes' drawn y values exceed h and are rendered off the bottom of the fixed canvas. Only nodes with very small node.y remain visible, all clustered at the top.
fix: Added wrapY helper in drawNetwork: wrapY = (y) => ((y % h) + h) % h. Applied to all drawn y positions (nodes, edge endpoints, cursor glow, spark interpolation). Also skip edges and sparks where the two wrapped y endpoints are >= CONFIG.maxDist apart (those pairs crossed the wrap boundary and would draw a long diagonal line).
